hero.name: Talvin Ackbaraly
hero.headline: Engineering student specialized in Computer Vision, AI & GPU Computing
hero.status: Looking for a 6-month internship — Starting early Feb. 2027

about.title: About Me
about.intro: As an engineering student at EPITA, I specialize in computer vision, artificial intelligence, and GPU computing. Following a first internship in full-stack & DevOps development completed during my core curriculum, I am now looking to apply my specialized skills during a 6-month final-year internship starting in early February 2027.

education.description: Image processing & synthesis, artificial intelligence, and GPU computing.

education.degree: High School Diploma — Majors in Mathematics & CS

items.title: Full-Stack & DevOps Developer
items.description: Development of an internal web platform for managing movie catalog rights, contracts, and distribution modules. Built full-stack features and set up CI/CD cloud infrastructure on AWS within an Agile team.

items.title: IT Consultant
items.description: Design and development of a management application prototype (suppliers, products, contracts) for enterprise client Sonepar using the Django framework and applying the Agile SAFe methodology.

items.name: PFEE — BNF
items.title: Segmentation & Classification of Heritage Illustrations
items.description: Final-year research project in partnership with the Bibliothèque nationale de France (BNF). The goal is to automatically detect and classify illustrations (engravings, figures, ornaments) extracted from digitized heritage documents.
highlights: Benchmarked object detection models: YOLO, Florence-2
Defined and tracked evaluation metrics (mAP, IoU)
Dataset management: annotation, cleaning, augmentation
Weekly client meetings with BNF teams

items.name: ALPR
items.title: Automatic License Plate Recognition
items.description: A complete license plate reading system (ALPR) developed in a team of two. Two approaches were compared: classic image processing and Machine Learning, with rigorous benchmarks.
highlights: Classic approach: filters, morphology, segmentation
ML approach: classification achieving 86% accuracy
Comparative benchmarks Python vs C++ on Train/Val/Test datasets
Full GitLab workflow: issues, merge requests, CI

items.name: IRGPU
items.title: Real-Time Video Motion Detection — GPU Port
items.description: Porting of a real-time video motion detection algorithm from CPU to GPU. The goal was to identify performance bottlenecks and maximize throughput using CUDA.
highlights: CPU → GPU port achieving a ×24 performance gain
Bottleneck analysis with Nsight
Optimization of memory transfers and CUDA kernels

items.name: MedViz
items.title: Medical Prediction & 3D Visualization
items.description: Prediction of Forced Vital Capacity (FVC) from pulmonary CT scans. The project covers the full pipeline: lung segmentation, feature extraction, ML prediction, and 3D visualization in a web interface.
highlights: Lung segmentation and 3D reconstruction from CT scans
FVC prediction using XGBoost
Web interface with Next.js for results visualization
Docker containerization and GitLab CI pipeline

items.name: POGL
items.title: Real-Time Fluid Simulation
items.description: A fluid simulation engine based on the SPH (Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics) algorithm, rendered in real time with OpenGL. All physics computations are offloaded to the GPU via Compute Shaders.
highlights: Real-time SPH simulation of thousands of particles
GPU physics via Compute Shaders (SSBO)
Rendering via Frame Buffer Objects (FBO)

items.name: TinyX
items.title: Social Network Clone
items.description: Development of a full Twitter-like social platform in a team of 14, simulating real-world engineering conditions with rigorous DevOps practices.
highlights: Microservices architecture: Java/Quarkus backend, React/Next.js frontend, MongoDB
Features: authentication, timeline, likes, follows, real-time interactions
Coordination across 14 developers with code reviews and project management

items.name: Tiger
items.title: Full Compiler Construction
items.description: Implementation of a complete compiler for the Tiger language, covering all classical compilation passes from lexical analysis to type checking.
highlights: Lexical and syntax analysis (Flex / Bison)
Variable binding resolution
Type inference and type checking

items.name: OCR Sudoku
items.title: Digit Recognition & Sudoku Solver
items.description: A complete image processing pipeline: detecting a Sudoku grid in a photo, recognizing digits with a custom-trained neural network, and automatically solving the puzzle.
highlights: Grid detection using classical image processing
Custom CNN trained from scratch for digit recognition
Integrated automatic Sudoku solver

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.text: I am actively looking for a 6-month final-year internship starting in early February 2027. Feel free to reach out to discuss opportunities.
